title: Kadir (Tarisian)
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Kadir}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Kadir11.jpg]] |name=Kadir |homeworld=[[Taris/Legends|Taris]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Gray]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|My name's Kadir. I'm the janitor for this building.|Kadir, to Revan|}} '''Kadir''' was an elderly [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who worked as a [[Janitor/Legends|janitor]] in the [[Upper City]]'s [[South Apartments]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] in [[3956 BBY]]. The quality of Kadir's work was often complained about by the tenants of the apartments, but he claimed to do his best with the equipment available to him. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], he encountered former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], who had the memories of a [[Republic soldier]]. Kadir answered Revan's questions and directed him to local areas of interest. ==Biography== ===Early life=== {{Quote|You also might want to check out the cantina. I used to go there when I was younger. Good place to get a drink and find out what's going on in the Upper City.|Kadir, to Revan|}} [[File:Uppercitycantina.jpg|thumb|left|300px|When he was young, Kadir enjoyed spending time at the Upper City Cantina.]] Kadir was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], where he lived in the [[Upper City]]. During his younger [[Standard year/Legends|years]], he frequented the [[Upper City Cantina]], which he viewed as a good place to get a [[Alcohol/Legends|drink]] and hear about current events in the Upper City. Aware of the [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gangs]] [[Bek–Vulkar gang war|running rampant]] in the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis's]] lower levels and content with his life in the Upper City, Kadir avoided the planet's [[Lower City]]. Kadir never left his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and eventually found himself working as a [[Janitor/Legends|janitor]] in the Upper City's [[South Apartments]] complex ring. The complex was occupied by [[Alien/Legends|non-humans]] forced to live there illegally by Taris's [[Speciesism|speciesist]] policies. The residents of the apartments were generally wary of outsiders asking questions, but were not bothered by them speaking to Kadir, as the residents viewed him as harmless and bordering on senile. Despite this, Kadir often received complaints about the quality of his work. This frustrated the [[Gender/Legends|man]], as he claimed to do the best he could with the few resources at his disposal, and his requests for better equipment and an [[Aide/Legends|assistant]] went unanswered. ===Jedi Civil War=== {{Quote|What do you think of the Sith and this quarantine?''"
"''Not much of an issue for me, you know? I ain't never left Taris and I ain't never going to. A lot of other folks is sure riled up about it, but I don't really know much about it.|Revan and Kadir|}} [[File:KadirApartments.png|thumb|right|300px|Kadir performs his duties as a janitor in the South Apartments.]] In [[3956 BBY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' in [[3956 BBY]]. during the [[Jedi Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]], the Republic [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] ''[[Endar Spire]]'' was [[Attack on the Endar Spire|attacked]] [[Orbit/Legends|above]] Taris by the [[Sith armada (Jedi Civil War)|Sith fleet]]. Following the battle, several [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] from the ''Spire'' crashed across the surface of Taris. One of these pods contained [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Bastila Shan]], who was an important part of the Republic war effort due to her powerful [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] [[Force power/Legends|ability]]. Wanting to prevent her escape, the [[Sith military (Jedi Civil War)|Sith military]] [[Capture of Taris|occupied]] the planet, declaring martial law and quarantining the entire world. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}} Several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] later, Kadir was approached by the [[Amnesia/Legends|amnesiac]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], a survivor of the battle overhead who had the constructed memories of a [[Republic soldier]]. Revan had been directed to Kadir by [[Larrim]], a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Merchant/Legends|vendor]] who ran a [[Larrim's kiosk|kiosk]] in the apartments and had informed Revan of the locals' misgivings of outsiders. Kadir initially warned him''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' not to walk on the floors that had just been cleaned, but eventually agreed to answer Revan's questions about himself and the planet, recognizing him as an off-worlder trapped by the quarantine and the new tenant of an [[Revan's hideout|abandoned apartment]]. Kadir advised Revan to avoid the Lower City and gave him directions to both the Upper City Cantina and the adjacent [[Kebla Yurt's Equipment Emporium|equipment emporium]] run by the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Kebla Yurt]]. When asked about the ongoing quarantine, Kadir acknowledged the frustrations of the trapped off-worlders, but explained that it was not a problem for him. He again directed Revan to the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], explaining his lack of knowledge on the subject and letting Revan know that people there would be more willing to talk about the topic. {{Endgame}} Eventually, the incumbent [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Darth Malak]] grew frustrated with the length of the search for Shan, and ordered the orbiting [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to [[Destruction of Taris|decimate]] the [[city]]-world. The [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] destroyed the vast majority of the Upper City, where Kadir lived and worked. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Hmph! You'll change your tune the first time your plumbin' breaks down and raw sewage floods your apartment. Then you'll be beggin' for Kadir to come help you out.|An offended Kadir|}} Kadir was an elderly Human male with [[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and light, wrinkled skin. He had receding short, gray [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. Kadir was content with his life and job on Taris. He never left the planet and never planned to do so, causing him to be unconcerned during the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] occupation and quarantine. Kadir was friendly to others and attempted to help them, even with his self-admitted lack of knowledge. He was perceived by the residents of the South Apartments to be harmless and near-senile, thus not drawing attention, despite the residents' general mistrust of outsiders. As a janitor, Kadir would grow frustrated when his work ethic or the quality of his work was looked down upon, such as someone insulting him or walking across floors he had recently cleaned. He was annoyed by his requests for better equipment and an assistant being consistently denied, knowing he was already doing his best but was still receiving complaints. ==Equipment== Kadir wore a white and blue [[Clothing/Legends|tunic]] with beige pants and a brown [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]]. He also wore green [[Glove/Legends|gloves]], brown [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and had minimal janitorial equipment that he used to the best of his ability. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|This place is a dump! You should be fired!''"
"''Hey, I do the best I can with the equipment I got! You're just like all the rest, complaining about me all the time.|Revan insults Kadir in a possible dialogue option|}} Kadir appeared as a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', developed by [[BioWare]]. Interactions with Kadir are optional and do not affect plot, quests, or alignment. Some potential dialogue options upset the man, and if Republic [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] [[Carth Onasi]] is in the player's party when these options are selected, he will chide Revan for antagonizing Kadir and asks them if they would like to live in filth. If the player speaks with Kadir again, they can ask Kadir who he is, causing the latter to display frustration at Revan not remembering him. After upsetting Kadir, the player may return to him and apologize, causing Kadir to assure Revan there were no hard feelings. ==Appearances== {{Indexpage}} {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Kadir (Tarisian)}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Kadir (Hausmeister) |es=Kadir (tarisiano) |pl=Kadir (Taris) }} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Janitors]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Tarisians]]
